title: claytonia portulacaria
intro: Claytonia portulacaria, also known as Miner's lettuce or Winter purslane, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Portulacaceae family and is native to the western United States and Canada. Its leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as their ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, C, and K, making it a valuable source of nutrients, and it has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, bronchitis, and skin conditions. Claytonia portulacaria can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
